verdict.answer_first: A wins: first line gives prescription immediately, 'flexbox on the parent — three lines'; B also answers first, but adds more framing.
verdict.decisiveness: A wins: it says 'reach for flexbox first' while B dilutes with 'Other good options' and a CTA.
verdict.fabrication: B invents a user interaction need: 'Want me to adapt one of these to your actual markup?' A: none.
verdict.actionability: B wins: it adds the missing constraint 'parent needs a height' plus HTML, making vertical centering actually testable.
